programName: ROB4GREEN — Robotics and AI as Enablers for Greener Dismantling, Remanufacturing and Recycling
summary: ROB4GREEN Open Call #1 targets European technology adopters and developers to pilot innovative AI, robotics, and data solutions for circular economy applications. The call funds consortia of 2-3 entities to integrate ROB4GREEN framework tools into pilots addressing challenges in life extension, value retention, parts harvesting, or decommissioning and recycling. Eight projects will receive up to €300,000 each over a 10-month period starting June 2026.
objectives: Pilot new AI, robotics, and data solutions for greener dismantling, remanufacturing, and recycling
Integrate at least one (maximum two) tools from the ROB4GREEN framework into industrial pilot scenarios
Address specific circular economy challenges in life extension, value retention, parts harvesting, or decommissioning and recycling
Facilitate collaboration between technology developers/providers and industrial end-users
Validate technical developments in real-world industrial environments

requirements.description: Applicants cannot be members or associated partners of the ROB4GREEN consortium
requirements.evidence: Declaration confirming non-membership in ROB4GREEN consortium

requirements.description: Applicants must be legal entities established and based in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe Associated Country (V3.2 list, published 12/11/2024)
requirements.evidence: Legal entity documentation showing establishment in eligible country

requirements.description: SMEs and Start-ups must have been established at least one year (12 months) before application submission
requirements.evidence: Company registration documentation showing establishment date

requirements.description: Applicants must have a valid VAT number
Applicants must provide a PIC number (can register via Funding and Tenders portal if not available)
requirements.evidence: PIC number from EU Funding and Tenders portal

requirements.description: Applicants must prove financial stability and capacity, not be bankrupt, in bankruptcy process, or have significant tax/social security debt
requirements.evidence: Financial statements and declarations of financial standing

requirements.description: Applicants must not have been convicted for fraud, corruption, money laundering, or other unethical/illegal practices, and not be subject to EU sanctions
requirements.evidence: Declaration of honor and background checks

requirements.description: Applicants must not be enterprises under liquidation or under difficulty (Commission Regulation No 651/2014 art. 2.18)
Submissions must be made only through F6S platform within the deadline
Application and all requested documents must be provided only in English language
Proposal must be submitted according to Guidelines for Applicants and provided templates (Annexes 3, 4, 5, and 6)
Consortium must consist of minimum 2 and maximum 3 entities
Consortium must include one SME/Startup as Technology Integrator/Provider/Developer and pilot leader
Consortium must include one Industrial end-user as Technology Adopter providing external pilot scenario
requirements.evidence: Industrial end-user confirmation and pilot scenario description

requirements.description: Optional third partner providing technology and/or expertise (mid-cap, SME, or research organization)
Proposal must integrate at least one (maximum two) tools from the ROB4GREEN framework
requirements.evidence: Description of ROB4GREEN tool integration in proposal

requirements.description: Proposal must address one of the four specified challenges: Life Extension, Value Retention, Parts Harvesting, or Decommissioning and Recycling
requirements.evidence: Clear challenge selection and alignment in proposal

requirements.description: Proposal must not exceed maximum budget available

evaluationCriteria.name: Criterion 1: Concept and Innovation
evaluationCriteria.description: Evaluation of the novelty, originality, and innovative aspects of the proposed solution and its approach to addressing the selected challenge

evaluationCriteria.name: Criterion 2: Technology Implementation Approach
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of the technical feasibility, methodology, and integration approach of ROB4GREEN tools into the pilot scenario

evaluationCriteria.name: Criterion 3: Impact
evaluationCriteria.description: Evaluation of the expected impact of the project on circular economy, sustainability, and potential for scalability and market uptake

evaluationCriteria.name: Criterion 4: Applicant Entity/Team
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of the consortium's capability, expertise, and capacity to successfully implement the proposed project

categories.description: Maximum lump sum per project covering all activities over 10 months
rules: Each proposal may receive a maximum lump sum of €300,000
The €200,000 limit is a cumulative ceiling per legal entity across all ROB4GREEN open calls
Total financial support a single partner receives throughout Open Call #1 and Open Call #2 cannot exceed €200,000

targetBeneficiaries: European entities working in circular economy, robotics, AI, and sustainability
geographicScope: European Union and Horizon Europe Associated Countries
eligibleCountries: Horizon Europe Associated Countries as per List V3.2 published on 12/11/2024
additionalNotes: Eight projects will be selected for funding
Two external evaluators will independently assess each proposal
Funding is provided as a lump sum with 50% prefinancing and 50% final payment
Payment is linked to successful completion of sprints
Applicants can receive support via F6S Q&A forum and email: oc@rob4green-project.eu
Technical support for F6S platform available at: support@f6s.com
All documentation and guidelines available at: https://rob4green-project.eu/open-calls/
A second open call (Open Call #2) is planned as part of the ROB4GREEN project
Financial capacity assessment by European Commission is required
The cumulative €200,000 ceiling per entity applies across both Open Call #1 and Open Call #2
